steps.title: Identify the annual decay factor from the exponent structure
steps.content:
The function is $f(x) = 3,600(0.28)^{\frac{x}{12}}$ where $x$ is in months.

The exponent $\frac{x}{12}$ tells us that each time $x$ increases by 12 (one year), the exponent increases by 1.

This means the base 0.28 is the multiplier applied each year.

After exactly 1 year: $$f(x+12) = 3,600(0.28)^{\frac{x+12}{12}} = 3,600(0.28)^{\frac{x}{12}+1} = f(x) \cdot 0.28$$ So the equipment value is multiplied by 0.28 each year.

steps.title: Convert the decay factor to percentage loss
steps.content: If the value is multiplied by 0.28 each year, then after one year the equipment is worth 28% of its original value. This means it lost $100\% - 28\% = 72\%$ of its value each year. Therefore $p = 72$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
